2605.18143.title: Generative AI and the Productivity Divide: Human-AI Complementarities in Education
2605.18143.paper_summary: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is transforming how firms create, process, and apply knowledge, yet little is known about the heterogeneity of its productivity effects across users. We report results from a randomized controlled experiment in which participants-analogs of early-career knowledge workers-were assigned to self-study a technical domain using either traditional resources or large-language-model (LLM) assistance. On average, GenAI access significantly increased task performance, but the distribution of gains was highly uneven. Improvements were not predicted by GPA or prior knowledge, but by \textit{AI Interaction Competence (AIC)} -- the ability to elicit, filter, and verify model outputs. High-AIC participants realized outsized gains; low-AIC participants saw limited or even negative marginal returns. A scaffolding intervention (conceptual maps) reduced outcome variance, indicating that standardized workflows can mitigate inequality in AI-mediated performance. We interpret these findings through the lens of human-AI complementarities: GenAI raises mean productivity while introducing a new axis of capability inequality. Managerially, firms should pair GenAI access with short AIC micro-training and simple standard operating procedures to capture value consistently and avoid uneven adoption outcomes.
2605.18143.paper_summary_zh: 生成性人工智慧（GenAI）正在改變企業創造、處理和應用知識的方式，但對於其在用戶之間生產力效應的異質性知之甚少。我們報告了一項隨機對照實驗的結果，參與者是早期職業知識工作者的類比，他們被分配使用傳統資源或大型語言模型（LLM）輔助自學一個技術領域。平均而言，GenAI的使用顯著提高了任務表現，但收益的分佈非常不均。改善的預測並不是基於GPA或先前知識，而是基於\textit{AI互動能力（AIC）}——即引出、過濾和驗證模型輸出的能力。高AIC的參與者獲得了超額收益；低AIC的參與者則看到有限甚至是負的邊際回報。一項支架干預（概念圖）減少了結果的變異，表明標準化工作流程可以減輕AI介導表現中的不平等。我們通過人類與AI的互補性來解釋這些發現：GenAI提高了平均生產力，同時引入了一個新的能力不平等軸。在管理上，企業應該將GenAI的使用與短期AIC微訓練和簡單的標準作業程序相結合，以穩定地捕捉價值並避免不均等的採用結果。
2605.18143.author: Lihi Idan et.al.
